title: Silene coeli-rosa
intro: Silene coeli-rosa, also known as celestial catchfly, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its pink or white blooms and delicate, powdery coating. The health benefits of Silene coeli-rosa include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Silene coeli-rosa is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Silene coeli-rosa is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. This family consists of approximately 437 species. Silene coeli-rosa is further classified in the genus Silene, which comprises over 700 species. The genus Silene is often divided into several sections based on morphological characteristics. Silene coeli-rosa is a member of the section Silene section.
uses: Silene coeli-rosa, also known as the sky rose or alpine catchfly, has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Europe. The plant has been reported to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial for wound healing and reducing pain. Some studies suggest that extracts from Silene coeli-rosa may have antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, potentially useful for treating various infections and oxidative stress-related diseases. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Silene coeli-rosa for medicinal purposes. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the potential health benefits of this plant.

preparations: Silene coeli-rosa is also known as the Rose Campion or China Rose, and its herbal preparations are primarily used for medicinal purposes. The plant is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to help alleviate respiratory issues and reduce swelling.
